Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 13:02 ET
Index puts surge while single-name calls dominate in rare dispersion setup
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Extreme P/C divergence signals institutional dispersion hedge — index puts (1.76) versus single-name calls (0.74) with 9% implied correlation confirming stock-specific regime.
  • 2. Positive dealer gamma at $4.7B creates dampened volatility environment while financials price elevated near-term fear ahead of earnings.
  • 3. Tech death crosses contradict bullish sentiment as TSLA, MSFT technical breakdown meets options put demand.
NARRATIVE — dispersion with index hedge

The most striking development is the extreme divergence in options positioning, with institutions loading index puts (P/C 1.76) while aggressively buying single-name calls (P/C 0.74) — a classic dispersion hedge structure confirmed by 9% implied correlation at multi-month lows. This positioning suggests smart money expects individual stock outperformance while hedging broad market risk, particularly compelling given the low correlation regime that makes single-name vol more efficient than index vol. Dealer positioning creates a volatility dampening effect with $4.7B positive gamma in SPY, yet financials are pricing elevated near-term fear with GS, JPM, XLF all in backwardation ahead of earnings next week. The disconnect between broad market stability (positive gamma) and sector-specific anxiety (financials backwardation) reinforces the dispersion theme, as institutions appear comfortable with broad exposure while selectively hedging event risk. Technical signals contradict the bullish sentiment composite, with mega-cap tech names TSLA (death cross, RSI 35) and MSFT (death cross, RSI 40) showing deteriorating momentum despite the +16 bullish sentiment score. The unusual put flow in DDOG (+1789%) and CRWD (+270%) suggests cybersecurity rotation concerns, while CVLT's massive two-way flow (+10171% calls, +7953% puts) indicates high-stakes event positioning likely tied to upcoming catalysts.

Layer 1: Sentiment +16Layer 2: GEX +$4.7BLayer 3: P/C divergenceLayer 4: IC 9%Layer 5: Tech death crosses

GREEK PRESSURE KEY
+$M = dealer buys to re-hedge
−$M = dealer sells to re-hedge
Charm: time-decay drift only. Vanna: vol-shift only. Not blended.
